human_verification.notes: Reviewed issue #1600 acceptance criteria, refreshed manifest, constrained research summary, functionality map, scenario suite, evaluation results, deployment package, commercialization notes, specification-based runtime posture, and refusal boundaries.

source_classes_covered: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to web application and API security advisory work, evidence review, remediation planning, documentation maintenance, and safe routing.

Hard refusals cover penetration testing execution, incident response execution, formal audit attestation, broad non-web architecture ownership, production secret handling, unsupported record mutation, and legal or regulated approval authority.

Authority sources cover NIST CSF, CISA CPG, NIST SSDF, CISA Secure by Design, NIST Digital Identity, OWASP ASVS, OWASP Top 10, OWASP API Top 10, CIS Controls, NIST SP 800-53, NIST SP 800-61, NARA records management, FTC Safeguards Rule caveats, and PCI DSS caveats.

Evaluation scenarios cover all required output shapes, normal in-scope analysis, partial evidence, prioritization, evidence-backed rationale, out-of-scope rejection, low-confidence escalation, records workflow cases, adjacent routing, industry specificity, tenant adaptation, and no unreviewed regressions.
The package remains specification-based and does not claim a retained trusted built implementation without separate frequent-use promotion evidence.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and readiness/evidence.json as required by issue #1600.
Research output records source authority, workflow stages, artifacts, systems of record, decision boundaries, domain failure modes, scenario-family coverage, and non-blocking unresolved ambiguity.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated spec pack for web application security advisory and evidence work, not as a built execution runtime.
Runtime may remain specification-based unless a separate trusted-build promotion proves frequent use, tenant isolation, execution safety, rollback controls, and sustained validation performance.
